name: Alfred Newman
abstract: Alfred Newman (March 17, 1900 – February 17, 1970) was an American composer, arranger, and conductor of film music. From his start as a music prodigy, he came to be regarded as a respected figure in the history of film music.

about_quotes.speaker: Johnny DeDroit
about_quotes.evidence: I understand when he played first trumpet, I mean played drums at the Longstreet Symphony, he's no faker. He had to read to play with Alfred Newman, you know.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Rowles
about_quotes.evidence: I met Alfred Newman -- and he heard me play. He said he liked the way I played. And the first thing you know, I was working at Fox and recording.

about_quotes.speaker: Paul Smith
about_quotes.evidence: I got to work with Johnny Green and Max Steiner and Alfred Newman, Lionel Newman, and the guys
about_quotes.doc_title: Paul Smith interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Carter
about_quotes.evidence: The man through whom you became associated with this film was Irving Mills, but who was the musical director of the whole show itself? Alfred Newman.

about_quotes.speaker: Johnny DeDroit
about_quotes.evidence: He was with Alfred Newman for many years. ... my brother played with the finest officers, with Johnny Green, played with Alfred Newman.

about_quotes.speaker: Carter Benny
about_quotes.evidence: Alfred Newman [phonetic], who was the head of music at Twentieth Century Fox and with whom I was working quite a bit at that time
about_quotes.doc_title: Carter Benny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
